steps.title: Find the hypothesis's central claim
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that immature females will peer at permanent residents more than males do, because females stay in their home region while males disperse.

steps.title: Match this prediction to the data
steps.content:
Choice A directly shows females (individuals 6 and 1) directing higher proportions (0.67 and 0.59) at permanent residents than males (individuals 13 and 15, at 0.15 and 0.00)—exactly the sex-specific pattern the hypothesis predicts.

Choices B, C, and D either show a range without sex breakdown, compare individual pairs without revealing the sex pattern, or measure total events instead of proportions.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
